problem: Dramatic recurrence. The three-city tour restages the same insight three times: in Cleveland (scripted material fails, the material-as-costume realization), in Pittsburgh (material fails, 90 seconds of the pool story work accidentally, then the return to material kills it), and in Detroit (material fails, heckler disrupts, 8 minutes of unscripted talk work, then the return to material kills it). The verdict is identical each time: spontaneity works, planning kills it. Cleveland and Pittsburgh deliver the same insight Detroit does, but with less dramatic pressure and no new information. The chapter also repeats a phone-call-after-performance template: each city ends with Doug calling Simone, the calls following the same ladder ('How was it?' / 'Bad.' / 'Bad how?' / specific diagnosis / Simone's brief comment). Cleveland contributes no unique dramatic content that Pittsburgh and Detroit don't deliver better. Pittsburgh's 90-second pool-story moment is valuable but could be folded into the Detroit section.
rewrite_direction: Target span: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3422-3688. Option A (preferred): Cut the Cleveland section entirely (3422-3497, ~75 lines) and open the chapter with Pittsburgh.
